name: montezuma_room_boundary_priority_v1
description: Boost replay priority at room-boundary transitions in Montezuma to force learning of crossing behaviors out of the start room.
llm_notes: Montezuma alignment_rate=0.3924 with mean_entropy=2.434 and disagree_near_terminal_rate=0.8506, while eval episodes remain stuck at max_room=1 with key_collected_any=false. Oversampling boundary transitions targets the no-progression bottleneck directly.

name: montezuma_high_entropy_phase_extend_16000
description: Extend Montezuma imitation-only warmup to 16k steps to reduce persistent high-entropy action uncertainty before RL updates dominate.
llm_notes: Mean entropy remains very high at 2.434 (>2.0 uncertainty threshold) and latest eval trajectories repeatedly show q_entropy around 2.89 with no room progression beyond room 1. A longer imitation phase tests whether representation grounding is still insufficient at 12k.

name: defender_high_conf_disagree_boost_v1
description: Aggressively prioritize high-confidence disagreements in Defender to correct confidently wrong habits where human actions are much more rewarding.
llm_notes: Defender has alignment_rate=0.0434, high_conf_disagree_rate=0.9566, and disagree_near_terminal_rate=1.0; importantly reward_on_agree=16.8449 far exceeds reward_on_disagree=1.1278, indicating human actions are strongly superior and misaligned confident choices should be heavily replayed.
